lessons: Added non-working residents + non-work (passive) income contribution rates (266 minimum, 3,442 exemption, 6.92%/7% NI + 5.17% health). Data was NOT in the skill, sourced live from btl.gov.il (NotWorking + non-working rates pages) and kolzchut exemptions page.

Expert review caught 3 CRITICALs on the first draft: (1) non-working spouse pays 0 not 266 (Form 673, akeret bayit), (2) the 3,442 passive exemption does not stack on work income, (3) pensioners/retirement-age are exempt from the NI portion. All three fixed by scoping the base table and adding an Exemptions and exceptions override table.

Legacy backfill: built evidence.json from scratch (118 entries, 178 facts) for the whole BL benefits catalog. Benefit amounts sourced to the 2026 rate circular; some scattered amounts (1,752.33 maternity, 1,314.25 work-injury, income-support tiers) cited to their benefit pages but not individually re-fetched, only the changed contributions facts were Judge-verified verbatim.

persistent_fail / CARRY: the benefit-amount evidence entries for the non-changed legacy catalog should be Judge-verified against their benefit pages in a future cycle (this cycle verified only the changed contributions section + spot-checks).

Added employee rate table BY age and pension status (under-18=0, working pensioner=0, 67-70 non-pensioner=3.93/10.03, woman ret-67=3.95/10.24, disability=3.23/5.17), self-employed age-varies note, te'um (multiple-employer) coordination rule, and household-help employer track. All employee rates verified by rendering the official btl.gov.il employee rate table via Playwright (WebFetch garbled it across 3 tries). Self-employed-pensioner exact % and household-help exact % ROUTED to btl pages (not fabricated).

ROOT-CAUSE of why v1.4.0 (and all prior gates) missed the age brackets: the pipeline verifies stated values + audits coverage against a topic-checklist, but never enumerated the official rate table's full row set. Fixed in update-skill (new Phase 3g.1 Rate-Table Completeness Pass) + admin-creator (Phase 1.7 Step C.1) on 2026-06-13.
CARRY/minor: chofesh-lelo-tashlum (unpaid-leave employer-continues) rule not explicitly named — non-material edge, revisit if a user hits it.

Replaced routed household-help rate with the real figures captured by Playwright-rendering the correct btl page (employer NI 6.05%, worker 1.8%+1%=2.8%, total 8.85%; under-18/pensioner employer 3.6%). Principle: never route a number a browser render can capture. Self-employed age variants stay routed because the official self-employed rates page genuinely tabulates only the standard 7.7/18 (no age rows published there).

SOURCE ROT: the skill's primary anchor (btl.gov.il/About/news/.../hadasaidkonkitzva2026.aspx) 404'd, and 38 of 135 evidence claims (28%) pointed at it. Re-anchored to the durable January benefits circular PDF (Publications/benefits_update/Documents/hozerkizba2026.pdf) + per-benefit 'סכום הקצבה' pages. LESSON: never anchor a skill's fact base to a NEWS page; news items get rotated off. Anchor to the circular/publication index or the per-benefit page.

Two income tests were materially wrong and had survived every prior gate because the gates verify STATED values against a source, and nobody had re-fetched these two: old-age income test was 7,827/10,436 (matches no official figure; the truth is 10,113/13,484 full, partial to 14,402/20,082) and siyud was 12,536/18,804 (truth from 1.4.2026: 13,769/20,654 full, 50%-reduced bands to 20,654/30,980). BOTH were also described as cliffs when they are graduated bands. LESSON: an income test is a rate table, run it through the Phase 3g.1 completeness pass, not just a value check.

The '60% of the average wage' derivation of the 7,703 bracket was FALSE (7,703 is 56% of 13,769). From 2026 it is a statutory amount (base 7,522, CPI-indexed through 2028) per circular 1522. Any agent that recomputed it got 8,261. The live /tools checker hardcodes 7,703 so the bug did not reach it. LESSON: a stated value can be right while its stated DERIVATION is wrong, and the derivation is what an agent will actually use.

'Amounts update twice a year (January and July)' was wrong in 5 places: BTL reissues on 1 January only (mid-year reissues happen per-table, e.g. siyud on 1.4.2026). LESSON: mid-year reissue is per-benefit, so the skill must tell the agent to check the benefit page, not trust an update calendar.

RESOLVED the v1.5.1 CARRY: the self-employed rates page DOES now publish the age/pension rows (0.26/0.78 NI for minors and pensioners, flat 237/mo health off a pensioner's pension, 6.92/15.79 past retirement age). Last cycle concluded 'not published there' and routed them. Also captured the previously-routed ba'al-shlita and naturalized-over-62 employee rows. RESOLVED the v1.5.0 CARRY: chalat (employer pays first 2 months, employee pays the 266 minimum from month 3).

Found a whole missing payer category: early-pension recipients (4.25% / 11.96%, withheld by the pension payer) is one of BTL's five official rate pages and the skill had no row for it. LESSON for Phase 3g.1: enumerate the official rate-page INDEX, not just the tables you already know about.

Coverage: added second-tier programs that existed only as orphan rows in forms.md with no body text (alimony, personal-accident/dmei te'una 2201, vocational rehab, burial + death grant, study grant, discharged-soldier grant, volunteer injury) and a status/obligations layer (residency, contribution gaps, employer non-payment, equity grant, BTL debt, overpayment clawback, certificates).

SKILL.md hit the 5,000-word validator cap; the full per-category contribution tables were moved to references/contribution-rates.md with a compact totals table + explicit 'read the reference before quoting a rate for a non-standard payer' instruction left in the body.

CARRY: the deep rule-sets for residency determination, BTL debt enforcement/liens, and bilateral-treaty totalization are named and routed but not specified. Either expand next cycle or keep them explicitly out of scope.

CARRY: the /tools checker (bituach-leumi-contributions) models 4 employment types and does not cover early-pension recipients or self-employed pensioners. Rates it uses are unchanged and correct; consider adding those categories.

Eval (10 frozen queries, 70/30 split): holdout queries deliberately AVOID the literal phrase 'ביטוח לאומי' so they test the trigger surface rather than the name. Baseline 0.67 -> 1.00, zero regressions. The gain came from naming the newly-covered programs (dmei te'una, mezonot) and the payer categories in the description; without that, the new body content was unreachable by routing.

THE BIG PROCESS LESSON of this cycle: the first pass fixed SKILL.md and left the OTHER artifacts carrying the old numbers. Both the Judge and the Expert failed the skill on the same root cause: scripts/calculate_benefits.py still PRINTED the stale old-age income test (7,827/10,436) that the whole update existed to remove, and references/benefit-programs.md still carried a fabricated SHRM tier table (invented 100%/150% tiers, 7,304 for the 188% tier), stale income-support rows (2,295/3,295) and stale siyud levels. A skill is an ARTIFACT SET, not a markdown file: when a fact changes, grep EVERY file (script, references, evidence, HE) for the old value before declaring the fix done. The executable is the worst place to leave a stale number because the agent runs it and prints it next to the corrected prose.

The siyud 1.4.2026 reissue changed the CASH amounts too (L1 1,659 -> 1,705, L6 7,238 -> 6,448 foreign / 7,440 Israeli caregiver), not just the income test. The first pass updated the income test and assumed the rest of the benefit was unchanged. When a source says 'this table was reissued on <date>', re-fetch EVERY table on that benefit, not the one you came for.

The forms path in the skill used a HYPHEN (btl.gov.il/טפסים-ואישורים) but the live BTL path uses a SPACE (%20). Every form-PDF link in the skill 404'd, INCLUDING the Troubleshooting entry whose entire job was to recover from a dead form link. It had been wrong for many versions and no gate caught it because link-checking treated the 419 bot-block as inconclusive. Fixed; T355/T480/T1500/T7801/T2201 all now return 200.

evidence.json had 14 junk 'phone-*' entries asserting that *1500, *7801 etc. are BTL phone lines. They are markdown-bold FORM numbers (**1500**) that the verifier's regex reads as phone-star tokens; a prior cycle 'justified' them by inventing phone-line claims rather than labeling the extraction artifact. Replaced with honest noise-bold-* entries. LESSON: when the evidence verifier flags something absurd, fix the LABEL, never invent a fact to satisfy the gate.

Benefit amounts that look flat are usually BANDED. Survivors (1,381 for a childless widow aged 40-50, not the 1,838 single rate) and income support (1,661 is the age-20-24 registering rate, not the universal single rate) were both quoted as single figures. Same failure shape as the income tests. Rule for next cycle: for every benefit, ask 'what does this amount vary BY?' (age, children, caregiver nationality, registration status) and enumerate that dimension, exactly as Phase 3g.1 does for rate tables.

CARRY: the child-allowance supplement for income-support families (reportedly ~113 NIS for the 3rd and 4th child) could NOT be confirmed on any BTL page during this cycle and was therefore deliberately NOT added. Verify or discard next cycle.

CARRY: birth grant covers twins (10,514) but the script exposes only a boolean --twins flag, so triplets+ (a higher multiple-birth rate) are uncomputable. Low frequency; fix when the script is next touched.
CARRY: calculate_benefits.py's calculate_pension() gates on age >= 62 and will happily compute a pension for a 62-year-old man whose retirement age is 67. Prose is correct; the code path is not.

ROUND 3 (the panel caught 4 MORE criticals after the round-2 fixes): (a) women's retirement age was stated as '65 for women born after April 1960' when the official table reaches 65 only from birth-year 1970 (a woman born 1962 retires at 63) — the skill was telling women to wait up to 2 extra years; (b) income support was fixed along the AGE axis but not the FAMILY-COMPOSITION axis, so a 35-year-old single parent of two was quoted 2,076 instead of 4,049; (c) the script's unemployment duration ladder was wrong in 4 of 5 rows and misread 'dependants' (the official test is 3+ family members), understating a childless 45+ claimant by 37 days; (d) the 300-day / 18-month unemployment entitlement for women 57-67 born 1960+ was absent everywhere while the skill asserted a 175-day ceiling.

META-LESSON (the one worth carrying): fixing a benefit along the axis you were TOLD about does not fix the benefit. Income support was corrected for age and still wrong for family composition; the retirement age was 'hedged' with a calculator link while the stated cohort rule stayed wrong. For every amount, enumerate EVERY dimension it varies by (age band, family composition, dependants count, caregiver nationality, registration status, birth year) and check each one against the source table. This is Phase 3g.1's rate-table completeness pass applied to BENEFIT tables, not just contribution tables.

The unemployment calculator had an INVENTED rate model (flat 80/70/60/50 by salary band) that matched no BTL table and was blind to the under-28 (60% ceiling) vs 28+ (80% ceiling) split, overstating a 22-year-old's benefit by a third. Replaced with interpolation over BTL's two published tables and golden-tested against 6 published rows (5,000/22 -> 120.00; 5,000/50 -> 160.00; 10,000/50 -> 257.81; 20,000/50 -> 380.06 — all exact). LESSON: a script may not carry a 'simplified' model that contradicts a published rate table the skill could simply encode.

A fix can land in the arithmetic and NOT in the printed output: the day-126 cap was correctly applied to the total but the print line still hardcoded 367.17, so the script's own output contradicted its own total. When fixing a calculator, re-run it and read what it PRINTS, do not just diff the math.

ROUND 4: the 300-day fix was DEAD CODE. I added the parameter to calculate_unemployment() and the branch that uses it, but never added the argparse flag or the call-site argument, so it was permanently False and `unemployment --age 60` still printed 175 days. The prose said the rule existed; the executable could not reach it. LESSON: after changing a script, RUN the new path. A parameter with no flag is not a feature.

Round 4 also found: the pension script returned 2,762 for an 80+ couple (prose everywhere said 2,865); the deferral bonus was unbounded and would print +100% at --defer 20 while the script's own note said deferral is paid only up to 70; and the --dependents CLI help still said 'Has dependents', dropping the 3-or-more definition at exactly the boundary an agent reads. All fixed and re-run.

references/domain-checklist.md had grown lopsided: obsessive on contribution rates (because that is where the last incident was) and nearly silent on the benefits side, so NONE of the four criticals from round 3 were protected against future regression. Rewrote it with a 'Must cover (benefits side)' section that names, for each benefit, the DIMENSIONS the amount varies by (age band, family composition, dependants count, caregiver nationality, birth year). The checklist is what the next Expert pass anchors on, so a fact absent from it will silently regress.

ROUND 5: three more criticals, all the SAME disease (a banded amount quoted flat) on benefits nobody had re-derived: (a) general-disability family supplements are banded BY DEGREE (spouse 1,518/1,123/987/911, child 1,214/898/789/728) and income-conditional, so a partial-degree family was overquoted by roughly a third; (b) the disabled-child allowance has FIVE levels topping out at 9,126 and the skill said 'up to 3,820', underquoting the most vulnerable claimant in the file by well over half; (c) the 300-day women's cohort carries a day-176 cap of 201.03/day for ages 57-60, which the freshly-wired flag ignored, overstating a real claim by roughly a seventh. LESSON: a duration rule and its PAYMENT rule travel together; wiring one without the other is worse than leaving the flag dead.

I also shipped a REGRESSION while fixing the unbounded deferral bonus: I capped it using CURRENT age (70 - age), which zeroes the bonus for exactly the person who completed a deferral and is claiming at 70. The cap is (70 - RETIREMENT age). And the bonus COMPOUNDS on the seniority-inflated pension (BTL: '5% מסך הקצבה המלאה כולל תוספת הוותק'), it is not additive. LESSON: when you add a cap, test the boundary case the cap is FOR, not just the case that was broken.

STANDING RULE now written into domain-checklist.md: for every amount, ask what it varies BY (degree, age band, family composition, dependants count, caregiver nationality, birth year, number of babies) and enumerate every row; then re-run the script for one case per row. A boolean flag (--twins) cannot model a banded amount (twins 10,514 / triplets 15,771 / +5,257 each) — the shape of the flag is itself a correctness bug.

ROUND 6 found the OLDEST bug in the skill, and it had been hiding in plain sight since before this cycle: the seniority supplement (תוספת ותק) was documented AND coded as '+2% per year starting from year 11'. BTL pays 2% for EVERY full insured year counted from year ONE, capped at 50% (reached at 25 years). BTL's own worked example ('15 insured years -> 30%') settles it. The script under-paid every pensioner with fewer than 35 insured years: --years 15 returned a figure well below BTL's own worked example for the same input. Nobody caught it for six review rounds because the WRONG rule and the RIGHT cap were written next to each other and looked coherent: 'from year 11, capped at 50% (25 years)' is arithmetically impossible (from year 11 the cap would land at year 35), and the skill even printed 'most pensioners get the full 50%' under a formula that could never reach 50% before year 35. LESSON: when a rule and its cap appear together, CHECK THAT THEY ARE CONSISTENT WITH EACH OTHER. An internal arithmetic contradiction is a free bug detector and costs nothing to run.

Round 6 also showed the specific way a fix rots: a corrected banded TABLE was added to references/benefit-programs.md while the old flat one-liner ('Children supplement: +1,214 up to 2 children', 'Disabled child: up to 3,820') was left sitting directly beneath it. That is worse than not fixing it at all, because the file now contains both the right and the wrong answer and the reader has no way to know which wins. RULE: when you replace a figure with a band, grep the whole artifact set for the OLD figure and delete every flat restatement, then re-grep. Adding the right answer is only half the fix.

improved: נפלתי במדרגות בבית ואני לא מתפקד שבועיים, מגיע לי פיצוי מהמדינה? (dmei te'una, previously unrouted: the old description named only work injury)
stable_success: איך מחליפים קופת חולים מכללית למכבי? (correctly NOT triggered, kupat cholim anti-trigger held)
אני עצמאי שמקבל קצבת זקנה, איזה אחוז אני משלם על ההכנסה? (routed before and after)
